hotpatch.note: Hotpatch month — enrolled Windows 11 Enterprise 24H2 · Windows Server 2025 (Azure-enrolled) apply this security update without rebooting; all other systems receive the standard cumulative update (reboot required).

servicing.preview_note: Optional non-security 'C' (3rd-week) and 'D' (4th-week) preview releases are not tracked here.

remediation.summary: Apply the June 2026 Security Updates (118 affecting Windows clients; 165 affecting Windows Server; 1115 affecting other Microsoft products).
remediation.note: Critical-severity fixes included — schedule promptly.

steps: Managed fleets: deploy via Windows Update for Business or Microsoft Intune update rings with an install deadline and enforced reboot; validate on a pilot ring first.
Standalone/home devices: Settings ▸ Windows Update ▸ Check for updates ▸ Install, then restart.
Watch for application-compatibility regressions before broad rollout; keep the previous cumulative update on hand for rollback.
Schedule a maintenance window — servers require a reboot to finish installation.
Deploy via WSUS, Configuration Manager (SCCM), or Azure Update Manager; for failover clusters use Cluster-Aware Updating to patch nodes without downtime.
Role-sensitive hosts (Domain Controllers, Exchange, SQL): back up first and follow role-specific patch ordering.
After reboot, confirm critical services and the OS build number.
For offline/air-gapped systems, download the specific KB from the Microsoft Update Catalog and import it into your tooling.
Track deployment per ring and prioritise Critical and actively-exploited CVEs for emergency change.

remediation.summary: Patch Adobe to the vendor's fixed release (recent high/critical CVEs from NVD).
remediation.note: Critical-severity fixes included — schedule promptly.

steps: Identify affected versions in your estate (software inventory / SBOM) and upgrade to the vendor's patched build.
Managed fleets: deploy via your software-deployment tool (Intune, Jamf, SCCM, WSUS-imported) and enforce an app restart; remove end-of-life versions.
Subscribe to the vendor's PSIRT/security advisories so future fixes are caught early; prioritise internet-facing systems.

remediation.summary: Update affected iPadOS devices to iOS and iPadOS 26.5.2.
remediation.note: Apply within your normal patch cycle.

steps: On the device: Settings ▸ General ▸ Software Update — install iOS and iPadOS 26.5.2 and restart if prompted.
Managed fleets: push the update via MDM (Jamf, Kandji, Intune, Mosyle…) or a software-update enforcement / deferral policy with a deadline.
Confirm the OS build matches the fixed version afterwards; remind users that a restart is required for kernel fixes.

steps: On the device: System Settings ▸ General ▸ Software Update — install macOS 26.5.2 and restart if prompted.

remediation.summary: SimpleHelp SimpleHelp has a vulnerability on CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog — update to the vendor's patched release as an emergency.
remediation.note: 3 CVE(s) are being actively exploited — patch immediately, out-of-band if necessary.

steps: Upgrade to the latest vendor-patched version. Browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) self-update on relaunch — force a restart of the app to apply it.
Managed fleets: push the patched build via your deployment tool (Intune, Jamf, SCCM, WSUS-imported) and enforce an app restart; block older versions where possible.
Per CISA BOD 22-01, remediate by the catalog due date; given confirmed in-the-wild exploitation, treat as an emergency change regardless.

patches.title: Mirasvit Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer

remediation.summary: Mirasvit Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer has a vulnerability on CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog — update to the vendor's patched release as an emergency.
remediation.note: 1 CVE(s) are being actively exploited — patch immediately, out-of-band if necessary.

sections.audience: Mirasvit Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer (all installs)
steps: Upgrade to the latest vendor-patched version. Browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) self-update on relaunch — force a restart of the app to apply it.
Managed fleets: push the patched build via your deployment tool (Intune, Jamf, SCCM, WSUS-imported) and enforce an app restart; block older versions where possible.
Per CISA BOD 22-01, remediate by the catalog due date; given confirmed in-the-wild exploitation, treat as an emergency change regardless.

cves.impact: Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability
products: Mirasvit Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer

cves.impact: Linux Kernel Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres Vulnerability

remediation.summary: Update affected iPadOS devices to iOS and iPadOS 26.5.1.
remediation.note: Apply within your normal patch cycle.

steps: On the device: Settings ▸ General ▸ Software Update — install iOS and iPadOS 26.5.1 and restart if prompted.
Managed fleets: push the update via MDM (Jamf, Kandji, Intune, Mosyle…) or a software-update enforcement / deferral policy with a deadline.
Confirm the OS build matches the fixed version afterwards; remind users that a restart is required for kernel fixes.

remediation.summary: Update affected macOS devices to macOS 26.5.1.
remediation.note: Apply within your normal patch cycle.

steps: On the device: System Settings ▸ General ▸ Software Update — install macOS 26.5.1 and restart if prompted.
Managed fleets: push the update via MDM (Jamf, Kandji, Intune, Mosyle…) or a software-update enforcement / deferral policy with a deadline.
Confirm the OS build matches the fixed version afterwards; remind users that a restart is required for kernel fixes.
